text.title: design-tokens-fundamentals Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for design-tokens-fundamentals as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Semantic token holding a raw hex value: "color.primary": { "value": "#3B82F6" }
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When the brand color changes, every semantic token holding the raw value must be updated individually. Alias resolution breaks the single-source-of-truth guarantee.

failure-mode.description: Agents default to two-tier (global + component) token structures; the semantic tier must be explicitly requested.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without explicit constraints in the prompt, agents collapse the semantic tier and put intent-based token names in the component tier, breaking the hierarchy.

failure-mode.description: Generated token names often mix conventions (camelCase in some, kebab in others) within a single file.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents follow patterns from training data without enforcing a single convention; specify convention explicitly in the prompt.

failure-mode.description: Agents frequently produce semantic tokens that reference raw hex values instead of global token paths.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without schema validation in the prompt, agents copy values instead of creating aliases; validate with jq or Style Dictionary build.

failure-mode.description: Token generation without a design reference produces arbitrary spacing and color scales.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents cannot invent design values; always provide the actual design values as input.

failure-mode.description: Token sprawl: agents generate excessively granular tokens (one per component state) that defeat the scalability benefit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without explicit constraints, agents over-tokenize. Specify maximum number of tokens and minimum reuse count in the prompt.
